source.title: EU countries agree temporary €3 flat customs fee for small imported parcels (Euronews)

extractions.claim: EU agreed on Dec 12, 2025 to impose a €3 customs duty per item (by tariff heading) on parcels under €150, effective July 1, 2026. A €2/parcel handling fee follows from November 2026.
extractions.evidence: EU countries agreed to impose a €3 tax on all parcels under €150 entering the bloc, taking effect from July 1, 2026. The €3 duty is charged per item based on tariff classification, not per parcel. From November 2026, the duty is due to be accompanied by the introduction of handling fees at two euros per parcel.

verification.verifier_notes: EU Council decision confirmed by Euronews, WWD, Global VAT Compliance, and multiple EU policy sources

extractions.claim: 4.6 billion retail parcels entered the EU in the prior year (>145 per second), with 91% originating from China. Up to 65% are estimated to be undervalued on customs declarations.
extractions.evidence: 4.6 billion retail parcels arriving last year, more than 145 per second, with 91% originating from China. The EU estimates that up to 65% of low-value parcels are undervalued on customs declarations to fraudulently qualify for duty exemptions, costing member states approximately €1 billion annually.

verification.verifier_notes: EU Council figures reported by Euronews and Ecotextile News

extractions.claim: The €3 duty is temporary until the EU Customs Data Hub becomes operational (projected 2028), which will permanently abolish the €150 customs duty exemption.
extractions.evidence: The €3 duty will remain in place until a permanent solution is implemented, namely the planned abolition of the €150 customs duty exemption once the EU Customs Data Hub is operational. Current projections suggest the hub will be ready by 2028.

verification.verifier_notes: From Global VAT Compliance and Euronews reporting
